info.name: Job Apply API
info.description:
Complete API reference for the Job Application Agent web app (https://job-apply-corey.fly.dev/).

Auth model: stateless HMAC session cookie (`session`). The login and register endpoints set the cookie automatically; all protected requests must include it.

Two run types exist:
- **Application run** — produces resume, ATS resume, and cover letter DOCXs
- **Interview prep run** — produces a two-column 9-section reference card DOCX (role fit map, gap bridges, anchor stories, likely questions, differentiating edge, closing line, and more)

Both run types are async: POST to start → stream SSE progress → done/error event contains file names → download files.

Base URL variable: `{{base_url}}` defaults to `https://job-apply-corey.fly.dev`. Override with `http://localhost:8000` for local dev.

variable.description: Base URL. Change to http://localhost:8000 for local development.
UUID returned by POST /api/run. Set this after starting a run.
UUID returned by POST /api/prep. Set this after starting a prep run.
Google Drive folder ID returned by GET /api/gdrive/runs.

request.description: Liveness check. Returns `{"status": "ok", "storage": true/false}` where `storage` indicates whether Tigris S3 is reachable. No auth required.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Session-based auth using an HMAC-signed cookie. The cookie is `HttpOnly`, `SameSite=Lax`, `Secure`, and expires in 30 days. No CSRF token needed for same-origin requests from the frontend.

request.description:
Create a new account. Accepts `multipart/form-data` so the master resume DOCX can be uploaded in the same request as the account details.

On success, sets the session cookie and returns `{"ok": true, "display_name": "..."}`. Password must be at least 8 characters. Resume must be a `.docx` file.

formdata.description: Display name shown in the app header.
Used as login credential and Google Drive silo key.
Minimum 8 characters. Stored as scrypt hash.

formdata.value: Paste your full profile.md contents here
formdata.description: Voice guide used by Claude when writing resume bullets and cover letter copy.
Your master.docx. This is the source every run starts from.

response.name: 400 Email already exists
response.body: {"detail": "An account with that email already exists."}

request.description: Authenticate with email and password. On success, sets the session cookie and returns `{"ok": true, "display_name": "..."}`. Failed attempts return 401 with a generic message (no user enumeration timing leak).
Clears the session cookie and the Fly.io machine-affinity cookie. No body needed. Always returns 200.
Returns the current authenticated user. Useful for verifying a session is still valid, and for the frontend to display the user's name. Returns 401 if the session cookie is missing or expired.

item.name: Profile
item.description: Profile endpoints manage the user's display name, profile guide text, master resume DOCX, and password. All require a valid session cookie.

request.description: Returns the full profile for the current user: display name, email, profile guide text, whether a resume is on file, and the stored resume filename.
Update the user's display name and/or profile guide text. Both fields are optional — send only what you want to change. The profile text is the voice/story guide passed to Claude on every run.
Replace the user's master resume. Must be a `.docx` file of at least 1 KB. This becomes the source file for all future runs. Does not affect previously generated output.

Change password. Requires the current password for verification. On success, sends a confirmation email via Resend (if `RESEND_API_KEY` is configured) and returns `{"ok": true, "emailed": true/false}`.

item.name: Application Runs
item.description:
Start and monitor a resume + cover letter generation run.

**Lifecycle:**
1. `POST /api/run` → returns `run_id`, kicks off background thread
2. `GET /api/run/{id}/stream` → SSE stream; events are `progress`, `done`, or `error`
3. On `done`, the event payload contains `files` (keys: `resume`, `ats`, `cover_letter`) with filenames
4. `GET /api/run/{id}/files/{name}` → download each file

Runs are serialised server-side behind a lock — only one runs at a time. If a second request comes in while one is active, it queues and starts automatically when the first finishes.

request.description:
Start a new application run. The server fetches the user's master resume and profile from Tigris, then invokes the full Claude workflow in a background thread.

Returns a `run_id` UUID immediately. Use the SSE stream endpoint to follow progress.

response.name: 400 No resume on file
response.body: {"detail": "No master resume uploaded. Add one in your profile."}

request.description:
Server-Sent Events stream for a run. Each event is a JSON object on the `data:` line.

Event types:
- `progress` — `{"type": "progress", "message": "..."}` — one log line from the agent
- `done` — `{"type": "done", "run_id": "...", "framing_angle": "...", "folder_url": "...", "files": {"resume": "...", "ats": "...", "cover_letter": "..."}}` — run complete
- `error` — `{"type": "error", "message": "..."}` — run failed

Keep-alive comments (`: keepalive`) are sent every 30 s if no message is in flight.
Poll the status of a run without opening an SSE connection. Returns one of: `queued`, `running`, `done`, `error`. Useful for checking whether a run is still active after a page reload.

Download an output file from a completed run. The filename comes from the `files` object in the `done` SSE event.

Returns the DOCX as `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document`. Returns 403 if the run belongs to a different user, 404 if the run is not yet complete.

item.name: Interview Prep Runs
item.description:
Start and monitor an interview prep generation run.

**Lifecycle:** identical to Application Runs but uses `/api/prep/*` paths. The `done` event returns `files.prep` (a single DOCX filename).

The output is a two-column reference card with 9 sections: Know Your Interviewer, Role Fit Map, Gap Bridge, Development Framework, Anchor Stories, Likely Questions, Questions to Ask, Differentiating Edge, and Closing Line. Pass `interviewer` to calibrate sections 1, 6, and 7 to the specific person.

`round_type` must be one of: `Phone Screen`, `Hiring Manager`, `Peer`, `Technical`, `Executive`, `Panel`.

request.description:
Start an interview prep run for a given job, company, role, and round type.

Optional fields:
- `interviewer` — name and title of the interviewer (e.g. `"Dan Spencer — Recruiter"`). Used to calibrate sections 1 (Know Your Interviewer), 6 (Likely Questions), and 7 (Questions to Ask).
- `focus` — a free-text steer on angle (e.g. `"lean into the agentic AI angle, they mentioned MCP"`).

Returns a `prep_id` UUID. Follow with the SSE stream endpoint.

body.raw:
{
  "job_posting": "Paste the full job description here…",
  "company": "Acme Corp",
  "role": "Solutions Engineer",
  "round_type": "Hiring Manager",
  "interviewer": "Jane Smith — Engineering Manager",
  "focus": "Lean into the agentic AI angle, they mentioned MCP"
}

response.name: 400 Invalid round_type
response.body: {"detail": "round_type must be one of: Phone Screen, Hiring Manager, Peer, Technical, Executive, Panel"}

request.description: SSE stream for a prep run. Same event format as the application run stream. The `done` event payload: `{"type": "done", "prep_id": "...", "folder_url": "...", "files": {"prep": "InterviewPrep_Acme_HiringManager.docx"}}`.
Poll the status of a prep run. Same shape as the run status endpoint: `queued | running | done | error`.
Download the prep DOCX from a completed prep run. Filename comes from `files.prep` in the `done` SSE event. Returns 403 if the prep belongs to a different user.

item.name: Google Drive
item.description: Proxy endpoints for listing run folders and fetching saved job descriptions from the user's Google Drive silo. Drive is configured server-side; clients do not need Drive credentials.

request.description:
List all run folders belonging to the current user from Google Drive. Returns both user-siloed folders and legacy flat folders.

Each entry: `{"id": "drive-folder-id", "name": "Acme_SolutionsEngineer", "web_view_link": "https://drive.google.com/…", "source": "user" | "legacy"}`.

Used by the Past Runs panel and the Interview Prep dropdown. The Past Runs list auto-refreshes after each completed run — no manual reload needed.

Fetch the `job_posting.txt` saved inside a specific Drive folder. Used by the Interview Prep form to pre-fill the job description when a user picks a past run from the dropdown.

Returns 404 if no `job_posting.txt` exists in that folder.

response.name: 200 OK
response.body: {"job_posting": "We are looking for a Solutions Engineer who…"}

response.name: 404 Not found
response.body: {"detail": "No job posting found in this Drive folder"}
